Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Of Assignment Pursuant To Ccp 708.510(A), Application For Order Restraining Disposition Of Right Of Payment, And Order For Seizure Of Personal Property Pursuant To Ccp 699.030(B)
Off Calendar Feb-22-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Paul H. Duvall, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: King & Ballow
Dennis C. Birkhimer, Esq., for Defendant and opposing party: MB Recordings, Inc.


Hearing held on PLAINTIFF KING & BALLOW's Motion For An Order Of Assignment Pursuant To Ccp 708.510(A), Application For Order Restraining Disposition Of Right Of Payment, And Order For Seizure Of Personal Property Pursuant To Ccp 699.030(B); ruling as follows:
The referenced statutes are permissive (e.g., "the court may order"), and it is undisputed that King & Ballow last year agreed to cease collection activities in exchange for monthly payments. It is also undisputed that MB Recordings has since paid more than $28,000, with less than $65,000 remaining on the debt. The equities favor MB Recordings at this time; the motion is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. Judge Richard Ulmer. Not reported.

ORDER OF EXAMINATION ISSUED
Off Calendar Feb-22-2010 Discharged
